item.title: Sara Duterte ‘duty-bound’ to attend March 25 House panel impeach hearing — lawyer
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte is "duty-bound" to attend the March 25 hearing of the House Committee on Justice on the two impeachment raps against her, according to one of the lawyers of the second impeachment complaint.

item.title: PCG to deploy vessel to secure 4th ‘Atin Ito’ civilian mission to WPS
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) will deploy a vessel to ensure the safety of participants in the Atin Ito Coalition’s fourth civilian convoy to the West Philippine Sea, even as it stressed the initiative is independently organized by civil society.

item.title: What assistance does government provide to repatriated OFWs?
item.description: The government’s repatriation program generally covers the provision of air ticket, airport assistance, halfway home accommodation, medical assistance or referral, domestic transport assistance, and psycho-social counselling, based on the Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA) website .

item.title: 'It’s all about sacrifice': Filipino Muslims celebrate Eid'l Fitr in Manila
item.description: Thousands of Muslims and families gathered at the Golden Mosque early Saturday morning, March 21, to offer congregational prayers in observance of Eid al-Fitr, also known as the Feast of Breaking the Fast.

item.title: Sapul sa CCTV: 8-anyos, ninakawan ng cellphone, pera sa Sta. Cruz, Maynila
item.description: Ninakawan ng isang nakabisikletang lalaki ang isang 8-taong gulang na batang babae habang mag-isang naglalakad para bumili ng meryenda sa Sta. Cruz, Maynila, noong hapon ng Marso 11.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
